0.title: Configuration
0.content: This page covers the configuration options for the WHMCS ↔ Bexio Integration.

1.title: API Credentials
1.content: Bexio API Setup . | Log in to your Bexio account and open developer.bexio.com | Go to Personal Access Tokens | Generate a new API key | Copy the API key to the module settings | . Note: The Personal Access Token expires after 180 days. Make sure to set a reminder to renew it. WHMCS Settings . In the module configuration: . | Bexio API Key: Enter the key from Bexio | myAlpineLink License key: Your personal License key | . For troubleshooting common issues, see the Troubleshooting page.

2.title: Module Configuration
2.content: Product Relations . Add your Product Relations to the Module. | Select whether you want to set a Relation for Domains or a Product within WHMCS | . Product . | Select the WHMCS Relation Product | Select the Product in Bexio. | Select the Account in Bexio. | . Domain . | Select one Domain or all Domains you want to set to this Product and Account in Bexio. | . Note . You can also set a relation to all Domains in WHMCS. | Select the Product in Bexio. | Select the Account in Bexio. | .

3.title: WHMCS ↔ Bexio Integration Documentation
3.content: Welcome to the documentation for the WHMCS ↔ Bexio Integration. This documentation provides comprehensive guidance on integrating WHMCS with Bexio to streamline your business processes and enhance efficiency.

4.title: Overview
4.content: The WHMCS ↔ Bexio Integration enables seamless synchronization between your WHMCS system and Bexio, including customers, invoices, products, and more.

5.title: Getting Started
5.content: . | Installation and Setup | Configuration | Troubleshooting | . For more information, navigate through the menus on the left.

7.title: Installation and Setup
7.content: This guide will walk you through the installation and initial setup of the WHMCS ↔ Bexio Integration.

8.title: Prerequisites
8.content: Before installing the integration, ensure you have: . | WHMCS installed and configured | A Bexio account with API access | PHP 7.4 or higher | cURL extension enabled | A backup of your WHMCS files and database | .

9.title: Downloading the Module
9.content: . | Download the latest version of the WHMCS ↔ Bexio Integration module from myAlpineLink. | Extract the files to your WHMCS modules directory. | .

10.title: Installation Steps
10.content: . | Upload the module files to your-whmcs-path/modules/addons/bexio/ and your-whmcs-path/includes/hooks/ | Go to WHMCS Admin > Setup > Addon Modules | Activate the “WHMCS Bexio Integration” module | Configure the API credentials (see Configuration section) | Add your License key | .

11.title: Initial Configuration
11.content: After installation, you need to: . | Enter your Bexio API key | Set up synchronization settings | Test the connection | . For detailed configuration options, see the Configuration page.

12.title: Troubleshooting
12.content: Common issues and solutions for the WHMCS ↔ Bexio Integration.

13.title: Connection Issues
13.content: API Key Invalid . Error: “Invalid API key” . Solution: . | Verify the API key in Bexio settings | Ensure the key has the correct permissions | Regenerate the key if necessary | . Network Errors . Error: “Connection timeout” or “cURL error” . Solution: . | Check firewall settings | Ensure outbound connections to Bexio are allowed | Verify DNS resolution | .

14.title: Synchronization Problems
14.content: Data Not Syncing . Symptoms: Changes not appearing in the other system . Solution: . | Check sync logs for errors | Verify data mapping configuration | Ensure both systems are online | . Duplicate Records . Issue: Duplicate customers or invoices . Solution: . | Review deduplication settings | Manually merge duplicates | Adjust sync filters | .

15.title: Common Errors
15.content: “Permission denied” . | Verify WHMCS admin permissions | Check Bexio user roles | .

16.title: Logs and Debugging
16.content: Enable debug logging in the module settings to get more detailed error information.

17.title: Product Relation
17.content: No Product Relation has been configured. Please go to the Bexio Integration Settings page and add your Product Relation.

18.title: Support
18.content: If issues persist, contact support with: . | Error messages | Log files | Configuration screenshots | .
